matches.match_rationale: Families submit documents in mixed formats — scanned PDFs, photos of birth certificates, handwritten medical forms. OCR and form field extraction can digitize these into structured records automatically, eliminating manual data re-entry by admissions staff. This directly addresses the 'no centralized intake or verification' mechanism.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An intake portal where families upload document images; the system extracts key fields (name, DOB, immunization dates, emergency contacts) into a unified student profile, flagging illegible or missing fields for staff review.

matches.match_rationale: Once documents are digitized, entity extraction pulls structured data (student name, date of birth, guardian contacts, medical conditions, academic history) from the text, populating enrollment records without manual transcription. This removes the staff bottleneck of manually keying information from collected forms into the SIS.
matches.opportunity_sketch: Post-OCR pipeline that reads digitized form text and maps extracted entities to required enrollment fields, surfacing a completeness score per student so staff see at a glance what is still missing.

matches.match_rationale: The entire document-collection workflow — receiving submissions, checking completeness against a required checklist, sending follow-up reminders to families for missing items, routing complete packets to the appropriate campus coordinator — is a multi-step branching process that an automation agent can own end-to-end. This directly eliminates the 'staff chasing families' bottleneck and the 'repeated submissions due to lost documents' problem.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An enrollment agent that monitors each applicant's document checklist, auto-sends templated reminder messages at configurable intervals for missing items, and triggers an orientation invite only when the packet is complete — with staff receiving exception reports rather than doing manual follow-up.

matches.match_rationale: Staff spend time drafting individualized follow-up communications to families requesting specific missing documents. Text generation can produce personalized, context-aware reminder emails and SMS messages that reference the exact documents still outstanding for each student, reducing staff drafting time and improving message quality and tone consistency across campuses.
matches.opportunity_sketch: Automated generation of per-family reminder messages that list exactly which documents are outstanding and provide step-by-step resubmission instructions, localized by language preference and campus.

matches.match_rationale: Families frequently need guidance on which documents are required, acceptable formats, and how to resubmit — inquiries that consume staff time repeatedly across enrollment season. A conversational agent can handle these multi-turn interactions 24/7, walking families through requirements, answering questions about specific forms, and confirming receipt of uploads without staff involvement.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A family-facing enrollment chatbot (web or WhatsApp) that answers document requirement questions, confirms what has been received, explains what is still needed, and guides families through the upload process step by step.

matches.match_rationale: Multi-site operators have varying document requirements by campus type, grade level, funding stream (government vs. tuition), and local regulation. Constraint satisfaction can determine the exact required document set for each enrollment case given its attributes, preventing over-collection and ensuring no required document is missed — addressing the 'numerous forms with no centralized verification' problem at its root.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A configuration engine that takes a student's enrollment attributes (campus, grade, citizenship status, funding type) and outputs the precise required document checklist, reducing both family burden and staff verification effort.
